content.problemStatement:
"You know Walter, sometimes it doesn't hurt to have someone watching your back"

The Hitmen twins of the Juarez Cartel, Leonel and Marco Salamanca, often known as the "Cousins", have set off from Mexico in search of Walter White. They come across an informant, who only agrees to help them if they solve his puzzle of interesting numbers.

There are NNN interesting numbers AAA111,,, AAA222,,, ......... AAANNN consisting of integers from 111 to NNN.

The task is to find KKKththth smallest GCDGCDGCD among all GCDGCDGCD ((( AAAiii,AAAjjj))) for each (i,j)(i,j)(i,j), such that 1≤i<j≤N1 \leq i < j \leq N1≤i<j≤N.

Print the KKKththth smallest GCDGCDGCD and also find any pair having the resultant GCDGCDGCD (print their indices).


Input Format

The first line will contain TTT, number of testcases. TTT testcases follow :
The first line of each testcase contains two space-separated integers N,KN, KN,K.
The second line of each testcase contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​


Output Format
For each test case, print two lines.



On the first line, print the KKKththth smallest GCDGCDGCD.
On the second line, print any pair of indices (x,y)(x,y)(x,y) such that xxx != yyy and GCDGCDGCD ((( AAAxxx,AAAyyy))) = KKKththth smallest GCDGCDGCD.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For Test Case 1 :  KKKththth smallest GCDGCDGCD is 222, and GCDGCDGCD ((( AAA111 , AAA222))) = 222.


For Test Case 2 : KKKththth smallest GCDGCDGCD is 111, and GCDGCDGCD ((( AAA444 , AAA555))) = 111.
